descriptor: [sandbox:claims/05_claim_adjudication_decision_and_payment.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Adjudication - Decision and Payment Fields Fields commonly produced during claim adjudication and payment calcul

value.chunk: # Claim Adjudication - Decision and Payment Fields Fields commonly produced during claim adjudication and payment calculation. ## claim_status Final or current status of the claim, such as Paid, Denied, Pended, Adjusted, or PartiallyDenied. ## line_status Status of each claim line after adjudication. ## auto_adjudicated_ind Flag showing whether the claim was processed automatically by rules. ## allowed_amt Amount allowed by plan rules for a line. ## total_allowed_amt Total allowed amount for the claim. ## paid_amt Amount actually paid for a line. ## total_paid_amt Total amount paid for the full claim. ## denial_code Standard or internal denial/reduction code assigned during adjudication. ## denial_desc Simple reason text for the denial or payment reduction. ## bill_code Synthetic billing classification code used by adjudication rules. ## pricing_method Synthetic pricing method such as fee schedule, DRG, APC, or percent of charge.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/01_claim_create_features_core.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Create Features - Core Identifiers and Header Data Simple descriptions of key data elements used when a claim is

value.chunk: # Claim Create Features - Core Identifiers and Header Data Simple descriptions of key data elements used when a claim is created by a member or provider. ## member_id Unique ID of the person covered by the health plan. ## provider_id Unique ID of the doctor, hospital, lab, or facility sending the claim. ## group_id Employer or group contract tied to the member plan. ## policy_id Policy or benefit package ID that defines coverage rules. ## claim_id Unique claim number assigned to the full claim. ## claim_type Type of claim such as Professional, Institutional, Dental, or Pharmacy. ## svc_from_date Start date when service was given. ## svc_to_date End date when service was given. ## line_id Unique ID for one claim line. ## line_num Sequence number of the line inside the claim. ## proc_code Procedure or service code billed by the provider. ## rev_code Revenue code used mainly on facility or institutional billing.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/04_claim_intake_matching_and_exceptions.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Intake - Matching, Exceptions, and Attachments Additional intake fields used for matching, exception handling, a

value.chunk: # Claim Intake - Matching, Exceptions, and Attachments Additional intake fields used for matching, exception handling, and document intake. ## duplicate_check_ind Synthetic flag showing whether duplicate screening was run at intake. ## member_match_status Synthetic result of matching the submitted member details to enrollment records. ## provider_match_status Synthetic result of matching provider details to provider master data. ## intake_exception_code Synthetic code for intake edit failures such as missing field or invalid format. ## intake_exception_desc Plain-English explanation of the intake issue. ## attachment_count Synthetic count of documents received with the claim. ## document_type Synthetic type of attachment such as medical record, referral, or invoice.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/03_claim_intake_core.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Intake - Core Intake Fields Fields created or stamped by the claim processing company when a claim is received.

value.chunk: # Claim Intake - Core Intake Fields Fields created or stamped by the claim processing company when a claim is received. ## received_date Date the claim was received by the payer or claims processor. ## submission_channel Channel used to submit the claim, such as EDI, Portal, or Paper. ## clean_claim_ind Flag showing whether the claim passed basic intake edits as a clean claim. ## received_amount Amount recognized at intake for the claim or batch. In this synthetic pack, use total billed amount as the intake amount. ## intake_batch_id Synthetic batch ID used to group claims received together. ## source_file_name Synthetic source file or image name linked to intake submission. ## received_time Synthetic timestamp showing when intake logged the claim. ## ocr_confidence Synthetic confidence score when paper claims are scanned.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/02_claim_create_features_line_and_financial.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Create Features - Line, Coding, and Amount Fields Simple descriptions of line-level details and billing amounts

value.chunk: # Claim Create Features - Line, Coding, and Amount Fields Simple descriptions of line-level details and billing amounts captured during claim creation. ## pos_code Place of service code that tells where care was given. ## dx_code Diagnosis code that explains the medical reason for the service. ## units Number of services, visits, or quantity billed. ## charge_amt Amount billed by the provider for a line. ## total_charge_amt Total billed amount for the full claim. ## network_ind Shows whether provider is in-network or out-of-network. ## lob Line of business such as Commercial, Medicare, or Medicaid. ## region Geographic market or service region. ## platform Processing platform or claims engine used for the claim. ## funding_arrangement Shows whether plan is Fully Insured or ASO/self-funded. ## submission_channel How the claim is submitted, such as EDI, Portal, or Paper.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/06_claim_adjudication_controls_and_review.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Adjudication - Controls, Reviews, and Timing Review, policy, control, and timing fields commonly used during adj

value.chunk: # Claim Adjudication - Controls, Reviews, and Timing Review, policy, control, and timing fields commonly used during adjudication. ## edits_passed Synthetic list of edits passed, such as eligibility, coding, and duplicate edits. ## fraud_check_status Synthetic result of fraud, waste, and abuse screening. ## medical_necessity_status Synthetic outcome of medical necessity review. ## authorization_status Synthetic result of prior authorization validation. ## coordination_of_benefits_ind Synthetic flag showing whether another payer must be considered first. ## benefit_applied Synthetic description of deductible, copay, coinsurance, or non-covered benefit handling. ## adjudication_queue Work queue or rules path used for the claim review. ## adjudicator_id Synthetic ID of the claims examiner for manual review. ## finalized_date Date final adjudication decision was completed. ## paid_date Date payment was issued if payment was made. ## tat_days Turnaround time in days from receipt to finalization.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/08_claim_rework_operations.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Rework - Operational Tracking Fields Operational tracking fields used during rework handling and follow-up. ## c

value.chunk: # Claim Rework - Operational Tracking Fields Operational tracking fields used during rework handling and follow-up. ## call_count Synthetic number of calls received about the claim. ## contact_channel Synthetic communication channel such as call, portal, fax, or mail. ## reopen_date Synthetic date when claim was reopened for rework. ## revalidated_date Date claim passed validation again after new information was received. ## rework_owner_team Synthetic team currently handling the rework. ## rework_sla_days Synthetic SLA target for rework completion. ## final_status_after_rework Final status after rework is completed. ## root_cause_category Synthetic root cause bucket such as missing info, coverage issue, coding issue, or duplicate.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/09_claim_event_core.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Event - Core Event Fields Descriptions of the event-level fields that explain claim lifecycle activity. ## event

value.chunk: # Claim Event - Core Event Fields Descriptions of the event-level fields that explain claim lifecycle activity. ## event_id Unique identifier for a claim event. ## event_seq Sequence number showing event order within the claim lifecycle. ## event_type Type of event, such as Received, Eligibility+Edits, Pended, Info Received, Routed, Auto Adjudicated, Manual Adjudicated, Payment Issued, or Denial Notified. ## event_ts Date and time when the event happened. ## event_actor Who performed the event, such as System, Provider, Examiner, RulesEngine, or Finance. ## status_after Claim status immediately after the event. ## note Free-text note that adds context for the event.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/07_claim_rework_core.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Rework - Core Rework Fields Fields created when a claim is reopened, corrected, appealed, or otherwise reworked.

value.chunk: # Claim Rework - Core Rework Fields Fields created when a claim is reopened, corrected, appealed, or otherwise reworked. ## rework_ind Synthetic flag showing that claim entered rework after initial processing. ## rework_reason Reason the claim was sent back for more work. ## rework_source Who triggered rework, such as provider, member, system edit, appeal team, or auditor. ## rework_received_date Date rework request or missing information was received. ## info_request_date Date payer asked for more information. ## info_received_date Date additional information was received. ## appeal_ind Flag showing whether a formal appeal was filed. ## appeal_level Synthetic level of appeal such as first-level or second-level.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/10_claim_event_extended.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Event - Extended Event Attributes Additional event attributes useful for reasoning over timelines and operations

value.chunk: # Claim Event - Extended Event Attributes Additional event attributes useful for reasoning over timelines and operations. ## event_category Synthetic higher-level category such as Intake, Review, Rework, Adjudication, Payment, or Notification. ## event_outcome Synthetic simple outcome label such as success, hold, denial, paid, or update. ## event_sla_bucket Synthetic SLA bucket such as same day, 1-3 days, 4-7 days, or over 7 days.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/37_claim_denial_gap_member_explanation_core.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Denial Gap Guidance - Core Missing Information Patterns Synthetic guidance to help explain common denial or pend

value.chunk: # Claim Denial Gap Guidance - Core Missing Information Patterns Synthetic guidance to help explain common denial or pend causes to a member in simple English. - **Missing member ID or coverage mismatch**: Your claim could not be matched clearly to your health plan enrollment. - **Missing provider credentials**: The claim did not have enough provider details to confirm who gave the service. - **Missing diagnosis code**: The medical reason for the service was not included or was not valid. - **Missing procedure details**: The service code, units, or billed details were incomplete. - **Missing authorization**: The service may have required approval before care was given. - **Missing referral**: A referral from your primary doctor may have been required. - **Duplicate submission**: The same claim or service appears to have been sent more than once. - **Coverage limitation**: The service may not be covered under the member plan or benefit package.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/38_claim_denial_gap_member_explanation_documents.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Denial Gap Guidance - Documents That May Be Needed Synthetic document gaps that can be used to explain what is s

value.chunk: # Claim Denial Gap Guidance - Documents That May Be Needed Synthetic document gaps that can be used to explain what is still needed. - **Medical records**: Clinical notes or chart pages supporting why the service was needed. - **Itemized bill**: Detailed provider bill showing each service and charge. - **Referral form**: Referral document from the treating or primary provider. - **Authorization letter**: Proof that the service was approved before treatment. - **Coordination of benefits form**: Information about other insurance when more than one payer exists. - **Accident details**: Information needed when injuries may involve another liable party. - **Corrected claim form**: A corrected submission fixing missing or wrong fields. - **Member statement**: Additional explanation from the member when requested.

value.chunk: # Claim Lookup - Network Indicators Simple meanings of network flags. - **IN**: In-network provider under the member benefit arrangement. - **OON**: Out-of-network provider that may be paid differently or at reduced benefit.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/39_claim_denial_reason_playbook.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Denial Reason Playbook Simple member-friendly explanation patterns for denial codes seen in the sample data. - *

value.chunk: # Claim Denial Reason Playbook Simple member-friendly explanation patterns for denial codes seen in the sample data. - **CO-16**: The claim is missing information or has something invalid that must be corrected. - **CO-18**: The service looks like it was already billed before, so it may be a duplicate. - **CO-50**: The service may not meet medical necessity rules based on plan policy. - **CO-97**: The service may not be covered by the member benefit plan. - **CO-151**: The claim amount was adjusted rather than fully paid at the billed amount. - **PR-1**: Part of the cost is member responsibility, such as deductible or cost share.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/44_claim_auto_vs_manual_adjudication.md chunk 1/1] # Auto vs Manual Adjudication Simple explanation of how claims may be processed. - **auto_adjudicated_ind = 1**: Claim w

value.chunk: # Auto vs Manual Adjudication Simple explanation of how claims may be processed. - **auto_adjudicated_ind = 1**: Claim was processed mainly by rules with little or no human touch. - **auto_adjudicated_ind = 0**: Claim required manual review or examiner action. - **Auto adjudication**: Usually faster for standard claims that pass rules and edits. - **Manual adjudication**: Usually used for complex, exception, or policy-sensitive claims.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/42_claim_actor_dictionary.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Actor Dictionary Simple meanings of actors seen in claim events. - **System**: Automated system process or workf

value.chunk: # Claim Actor Dictionary Simple meanings of actors seen in claim events. - **System**: Automated system process or workflow engine. - **RulesEngine**: Automated adjudication or edit engine. - **Provider**: Provider or provider office sent new information. - **Examiner**: Human claims examiner or processor. - **Finance**: Payment processing or treasury function.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/43_claim_clean_claim_logic.md chunk 1/1] # Clean Claim Logic Simple explanation of what a clean claim indicator usually means. - **clean_claim_ind = 1**: Claim p

value.chunk: # Clean Claim Logic Simple explanation of what a clean claim indicator usually means. - **clean_claim_ind = 1**: Claim passed basic format and required-field checks on first receipt. - **clean_claim_ind = 0**: Claim needed extra handling, had exceptions, or failed one or more intake or validation checks. - **Note**: A clean claim can still later deny, pend, or adjust based on deeper adjudication rules.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/40_claim_lifecycle_overview.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Lifecycle Overview High-level stages that a US health claim may pass through. - **Claim creation**: Provider or

value.chunk: # Claim Lifecycle Overview High-level stages that a US health claim may pass through. - **Claim creation**: Provider or member builds the claim with services, dates, and billing details. - **Intake**: Payer receives the claim and performs format, member, and provider checks. - **Validation and edits**: System runs eligibility, coding, duplicate, and policy edits. - **Pend or rework**: Claim waits for missing information, correction, or further review. - **Adjudication**: Rules engine or examiner determines allowed amount, payment, or denial. - **Notification**: Provider and member receive remittance or denial information. - **Payment**: Approved payment is issued to the provider or member. - **Appeal or adjustment**: Claim can be reopened for appeal, correction, or post-payment review.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/41_claim_status_dictionary.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Status Dictionary Simple meanings of claim and line statuses found in the data. - **Received**: Claim has been a

value.chunk: # Claim Status Dictionary Simple meanings of claim and line statuses found in the data. - **Received**: Claim has been accepted into the processor system. - **Validated**: Basic edits or checks have been completed. - **InReview**: Claim is in a queue for manual or rules review. - **Pended**: Claim is waiting for missing information or a decision. - **Paid**: Claim or line has been approved and paid. - **Denied**: Claim or line has been rejected with no payment. - **PartiallyDenied**: Some services were paid and some were denied or reduced. - **Adjusted**: An earlier decision or amount was corrected or recalculated. - **Closed**: Processing cycle for the claim has been completed. - **Rework**: Claim returned for correction or additional handling.

value.chunk: # Claim Timeline Fields Simple explanation of the main dates in a claim. - **svc_from_date**: Date service started. - **svc_to_date**: Date service ended. - **received_date**: Date claim was received. - **finalized_date**: Date claim decision was finalized. - **paid_date**: Date payment was issued. - **event_ts**: Timestamp of a claim lifecycle event. - **tat_days**: Total turnaround time in days from receipt to final decision.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/45_claim_financial_amounts_dictionary.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Financial Amounts Dictionary Simple explanation of common claim financial terms. - **charge_amt**: Amount billed

value.chunk: # Claim Financial Amounts Dictionary Simple explanation of common claim financial terms. - **charge_amt**: Amount billed by the provider for one service line. - **allowed_amt**: Amount the plan allows before payment and member cost share are applied. - **paid_amt**: Amount actually paid after rules and member responsibility are applied. - **total_charge_amt**: Sum of all provider billed amounts on the claim. - **total_allowed_amt**: Sum of all allowed amounts on the claim. - **total_paid_amt**: Sum of all payments made on the claim.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/47_claim_common_user_questions.md chunk 1/1] # Common User Questions on Claims Example user questions the AI agent should be able to answer with these files. - **Why

value.chunk: # Common User Questions on Claims Example user questions the AI agent should be able to answer with these files. - **Why was my claim denied?**: Use denial codes, denial guidance, and missing information topics. - **What does this claim status mean?**: Use status dictionary and event files. - **Was my claim processed automatically?**: Use auto adjudication indicator and event history. - **How much was billed, allowed, and paid?**: Use financial terms and claim metadata. - **Who submitted the claim and how?**: Use provider, member, and submission channel context. - **What information is still missing?**: Use denial gap files and rework fields. - **What happened next on my claim?**: Use event sequence and lifecycle overview. - **How can I correct or appeal the claim?**: Use rework and appeal tracking files.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/51_claim_ai_agent_usage_notes.md chunk 1/1] # AI Agent Usage Notes Instructions-oriented notes for an AI agent using this knowledge pack. - **Prefer plain English**

value.chunk: # AI Agent Usage Notes Instructions-oriented notes for an AI agent using this knowledge pack. - **Prefer plain English**: Answer business users with short, simple explanations first. - **Use claim level first**: Start with claim header status, dates, and totals before drilling into lines. - **Use line detail for denials**: If a claim is partially denied, inspect line_status and denial_code. - **Use events for chronology**: Explain what happened using event_seq and event_ts. - **Use rework guidance for gaps**: When data is missing, explain what likely needs to be corrected or sent. - **Be explicit on synthetic content**: State clearly when a lookup or advisory value is synthetic. - **Do not overstate certainty**: If the sample lacks a field, say it is inferred or synthetic. - **Preserve identifiers exactly**: Return claim IDs, line IDs, and codes exactly as stored.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/49_claim_data_quality_checks.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Data Quality Checks Useful checks when validating synthetic or real claim data for AI use. - **Identifier comple

value.chunk: # Claim Data Quality Checks Useful checks when validating synthetic or real claim data for AI use. - **Identifier completeness**: Confirm claim_id, member_id, and provider_id are present. - **Date order**: Service dates should not be after received date in most normal scenarios. - **Amount balancing**: Sum of line charges should align with claim total charge when expected. - **Status consistency**: Closed claims should usually have final status and dates populated. - **Denial consistency**: Denied lines should normally have a denial code and explanation. - **Event ordering**: event_seq should match the lifecycle order and event timestamps. - **Code validity**: Procedure, diagnosis, POS, and revenue codes should be in approved lookup sets. - **Duplicate detection**: Check for repeated claims or lines with same member, service date, and code.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/50_claim_joining_guide.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Joining Guide How the uploaded datasets relate to each other. - **claims to lines**: Join on claim_id to link cl

value.chunk: # Claim Joining Guide How the uploaded datasets relate to each other. - **claims to lines**: Join on claim_id to link claim header data to line details. - **claims to events**: Join on claim_id to link claim header data to event timeline. - **flat file**: Already combines line data with claim header data in one table. - **Primary key at claim level**: claim_id - **Primary key at line level**: line_id, with claim_id and line_num as context. - **Primary key at event level**: event_id, with claim_id and event_seq as context.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/52_claim_glossary.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Glossary Short glossary of common US healthcare claim terms. - **Adjudication**: The process of deciding how a c

value.chunk: # Claim Glossary Short glossary of common US healthcare claim terms. - **Adjudication**: The process of deciding how a claim should be paid, denied, or adjusted. - **Allowed amount**: The amount a plan recognizes for payment purposes. - **Appeal**: A request to review a denial or payment decision again. - **Clean claim**: A claim with required information that passes basic edits. - **Coordination of benefits**: Rules used when more than one health plan may pay. - **Deductible**: Amount the member must pay before certain benefits start. - **EDI**: Electronic Data Interchange used to send claims electronically. - **Pend**: A temporary hold while more information or review is needed. - **Remittance**: Payment explanation sent to a provider after claim processing. - **Utilization management**: Processes that check medical necessity and authorization rules.

descriptor: [sandbox:claims/53_claim_denial_prevention_tips.md chunk 1/1] # Claim Denial Prevention Tips Simple tips to reduce common denials and pends. - **Verify eligibility early**: Confirm m

value.chunk: # Claim Denial Prevention Tips Simple tips to reduce common denials and pends. - **Verify eligibility early**: Confirm member coverage before or at the time of service. - **Submit complete coding**: Include valid diagnosis, procedure, POS, and revenue codes. - **Check authorization rules**: Make sure required approvals are in place before billing. - **Avoid duplicates**: Confirm the claim was not submitted already before resending. - **Attach needed documents**: Send medical records, referrals, and corrected forms when required. - **Validate provider data**: Ensure rendering and billing provider information is correct. - **Review member cost share**: Explain deductible or coinsurance expectations early. - **Use corrected claim indicators**: When resubmitting, clearly mark the bill as corrected or replacement.
